title: Provider and Tool Errors, Retry Eligibility, Idempotency, Compensation, and Stop · Transfer Build
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers provider and tool errors, retry eligibility, idempotency, compensation, and stop into a changed typed idempotent agent recovery policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed idempotent agent recovery policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Classify validation, safety, authentication, authorization, quota, rate, transient provider, terminal provider, tool, policy, timeout, cancellation, and uncertain-effect failures.
Retry only idempotent or protected operations when policy, deadline, budget, status, provider guidance, and attempt evidence permit.
Apply bounded delay, Retry-After, jitter, global and per-resource concurrency, rate, token, and spend budgets without retry storms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 8307 during a clean package, canary, and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to classify provider and tool failures, retry only safe cases with bounded backoff, preserve idempotency, compensate, and stop visibly; sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments; then change model capability, API version, schema, or provider state and revalidate the adapter. steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compensation Stop
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-compensation-stop-ev3tnm" that uses case 5556 to classify provider and tool failures, retry only safe cases with bounded backoff, preserve idempotency, compensate, and stop visibly. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compensation Stop.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Compensation Stop into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Compensation Stop, keep Compensation Stop connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-compensation-stop-ev3tnm
# Competency: agentpy-compensation-stop.
# Case 5556: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: sensitive actions require a trustworthy user approval bound to exact arguments.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_compensation_stop_ev3tnm(failures: int = 18, retryable_failures: int = 8, terminal_failures: int = 10, successful_retries: int = 7, duplicate_effects: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ev3tnm = "ev3tnm-mnt3ve-5556"
    assert evidence_variant_ev3tnm
    evidence_axes_ev3tnm = (
        "authorityev3tnm",
        "interaction5556",
        "toolmnt3ve",
        "stateev3tnm5556",
        "evaluation5556ev3tnm",
        "recoverymnt3ve5556",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ev3tnm)) == 6
    if failures < 1 or retryable_failures < 0 or terminal_failures < 0:
        return False, failures, "failure-count"
    if retryable_failures + terminal_failures != failures:
        return False, retryable_failures, "failure-classification"
    if successful_retries < 0 or successful_retries > retryable_failures:
        return False, successful_retries, "retry-evidence"
    if duplicate_effects > 0:
        return False, duplicate_effects, "retry-duplicate-effect"
    assert retryable_failures + terminal_failures == failures and duplicate_effects == 0
    return True, successful_retries, "idempotent-agent-recovery"
